definitions.text: An item of information put into a temporary or permanent physical medium.
examples: The person had a record of the interview so she could review her notes.
The tourist's photographs and the tape of the police call provide a record of the crime.

definitions.text: Any instance of a physical medium on which information was put for the purpose of preserving it and making it available for future reference.
examples: We have no record of you making this payment to us.
My remarks were struck from the record.

definitions.text: Ellipsis of phonograph record (“a disc, usually made from vinyl, on which sound is recorded and may be replayed on a phonograph”).
examples: I still like records better than CDs.

definitions.text: Enough to break previous records and set a new one; world-class; historic.
To make a record of information.
examples: I wanted to record every detail of what happened, for the benefit of future generations.

definitions.text: To make an audio or video recording of.
examples: Within a week they had recorded both the song and the video for it.

definitions.text: To give legal status to by making an official public record.
examples: When the deed was recorded, we officially owned the house.
